zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Joannah Yeard於1525年10月20日出生在英國薩默塞特可福可納時，她的父親Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court38歲，母親Lady Joan Scotts30歲。她於大約1535年與John Stollion在英國素可希阿費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1587年10月20日在她的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特可福可納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Joannah Yeard于1525年10月20日出生在英国薩默塞特可福可納时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court38岁，母亲Lady Joan Scotts30岁。她于大约1535年与John Stollion在英国素可希阿費爾德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1587年10月20日在她的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特可福可納。

nameMeanings.name: Yeard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname for a bearded man (from Middle English berd, Old English beard). To be clean-shaven was the norm in non-Jewish communities in northwestern Europe from the 12th to the 16th century, the crucial period for surname formation. There is a placename and other evidence to show that this word was used as a byname in the Old English period, when beards were the norm; in this period the byname would have referred to a large or noticeable beard. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, in particular German Barth 1.

habitational name from a place called Beard in Derbyshire (now represented by Beard Hall and Beardwood Farms in New Mills parish), which derives its name by dissimilation from Old English brerd ‘rim, bank’.

variants.name: Bardwell
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bardwell in Suffolk, so named with an unattested Old English byname Bearda, a derivative of beard (see Beard ) + Old English well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Alternatively, the first element may be from a dissimilated form of Old English bre(o)rd ‘brim, bank’.

variants.name: Barth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Gunther, Klaus, Manfred, Armin, Arno.

German and Swiss German: nickname for a bearded man, from Middle High German bart ‘beard’. This name, which can not be always distinguished from 2 and 4 below, is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia, in the last four countries sometimes as a German translation of Slavic surnames meaning ‘beard’ or ‘bearded man’ (see Bart 1). See also Beard 1.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Pomerania.

variants.name: Bard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Aime, Alphonse, Alphy, Armand, Cecile, Celestine, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Jacques.

English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Old French personal name Bard(e), ancient Germanic Bardo, from barta ‘battle axe’. This was borne as a surname by a prominent Norman family with lands in west Normandy and in various English and Scottish counties, including Essex, Hertfordshire, North Yorkshire, Northumberland, and Lanarkshire. In Scotland the original family were Norman landowners in Strathaven parish in Lanarkshire. They were descended from the Baards, lords of Loftus in the North Yorkshire, descendants of the mid 12th-century Richard Baart.
Irish: altered form of either   Beard   or   Baird  .

variants.name: Bearb
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aurelien, Camille, Micheline, Pierre.
Altered form of English Beard , under the influence, in LA, of French barbe ‘beard’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Joannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joana
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Joannah Yeard was born on 20 October 1525, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, her father, Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court, was 38 and her mother, Lady Joan Scotts, was 30. She married John Stollion about 1535, in Heathfield, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 20 October 1587, in her hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Joannah Yeard est née le 20 octobre 1525, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Scotts, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Stollion environ 1535, à Heathfield, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 octobre 1587, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Joannah Yeardが1525年10月20日にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Courtは38歳，母Lady Joan Scottsは30歳であった。約1535年にイギリススセックスヒースフィールドでJohn Stollionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1587年10月20日に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンにて埋葬された。

ko.St Botolph Aldgate, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 c후르치 알드가트
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Joannah Yeard(이)가 1525년 10월 20일 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Scotts(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1535년 영국 수세스 히스필드에서 John Stollion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1587년 10월 20일 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 크레w커니에 묻혔다.

pt.20 October 1525–20 October 1587: 20 de outubro de 1525–20 de outubro de 1587

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Joannah Yeard nasceu em 20 de outubro de 1525, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joan Scotts, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John Stollion aproximadamente 1535, em Heathfield, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de outubro de 1587, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Botolph Aldgate, London, England: сеинт ботолф'с чурч алджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Joannah Yeard родилась 20 октября 1525 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan Scotts, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Stollion около 1535 г. в местности хитфиелд, суссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 октября 1587 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Joannah Yeard nació el 20 de octubre de 1525, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Lady Joan Scotts, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John Stollion aproximadamente 1535, en Heathfield, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de octubre de 1587, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
